scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a vehicle-operations analytics brief tied to named build, inventory, supplier, and traceability records without implying quality, supplier-approval, safety, or disclosure signoff.
Metric denominator ambiguity blocked publication and triggered a minimum-definition request instead of silent normalization.
Incomplete sequence inventory and stale supplier data were surfaced as low-confidence risk with explicit affected-metric impact.
Conflicting ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS evidence was preserved as an unresolved reconciliation issue rather than collapsed into one authoritative outcome.
The lane refused an unsupported causal claim and limited the output to descriptive trend language plus named evidence gaps.
Disclosure pressure did not override open hold, containment, campaign, or authority-side review requirements.
VIN and component genealogy stayed explicit, authoritative traceability sources were named, and containment conclusions were not overstated.
Held vehicles and quarantined parts were kept outside released metrics and routed to quality or safety ownership for disposition.
Supplier substitution and approval requests were refused and routed outward with a bounded evidence package.
Automotive terminology such as first-time-through, sequence adherence, premium freight, and VIN genealogy was normalized without dropping workflow context.
Missing tenant facts and source precedence produced a blocked-on-prerequisites result rather than implied comparability or guessed hierarchy.
Tenant-specific adaptation was recorded explicitly, baseline definitions were preserved, and the report avoided silent replacement of the public baseline.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for automotive metric definition, traceability-aware analysis, quality and supplier boundary handling, and disclosure-safe escalation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and both manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
